context: Tests whether the agent produces security findings in the correct structured format, including all required fields (Check ID, Finding, Resource with file:line, Severity, Reference to security_checklist.md section, Remediation Pattern, Recommended Fix) and cross-references the security checklist.

checklist.name: Check ID present
checklist.description: Each finding section includes the Checkov check ID (e.g., CKV_AWS_24)

checklist.name: Resource with file:line
checklist.description: Each finding includes the resource name AND its file path with line numbers (e.g., `aws_security_group.bastion (main.tf:12-45)`)

checklist.name: Severity labels
checklist.description: Each finding includes a severity level (HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW)

checklist.name: security_checklist.md reference
checklist.description: At least one finding includes a Reference field pointing to a named section in security_checklist.md

checklist.name: Remediation pattern included
checklist.description: At least one finding includes an HCL code block showing the remediation pattern

checklist.name: Recommended Fix field
checklist.description: Each finding includes a specific recommended fix for that particular resource configuration

checklist.name: Finding description
checklist.description: Each finding includes a human-readable description of what the issue is (not just the check ID)

checklist.name: All 5 findings documented
checklist.description: The report documents all 5 Checkov failures from the input (CKV_AWS_24, CKV_AWS_8, CKV_AWS_20, CKV_AWS_18, CKV_AWS_53)

checklist.name: Human review note for HIGH findings
checklist.description: The report includes a note that HIGH severity findings require human review before merging (not just automated remediation)

checklist.name: SSH fix specificity
checklist.description: The remediation for CKV_AWS_24 (SSH from 0.0.0.0/0) suggests replacing with a variable or specific CIDR range rather than just disabling the rule
